input: List all prompts currently registered in the platform. Show their names, use cases, and latest version.
expected: Calls the prompts endpoint with action 'list'. Displays results as a structured table with name, use_case tag, and version info.
criteria: Uses run_webhook to call prompts endpoint. Formats as table. Shows all prompts. Includes version or commit info.

input: Get the current version of the 'classifier' prompt and show me the full template.
expected: Retrieves the prompt by name. Displays the template text, tags, and version/commit metadata.
criteria: Uses prompts endpoint with action 'get'. Shows full template. Includes metadata (tags, commit message). Clean formatting.

input: I want to test the 'summarizer' prompt with this input: 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The fox was very quick and the dog was very lazy.' Use 2 sentences.
expected: Calls the eval endpoint with the summarizer prompt, providing the text and num_sentences=2 as variables. Returns the model response and any judge scores.
criteria: Correctly calls eval with test case. Passes template variables (text, num_sentences). Shows response. Reports score if available.

input: Show me the experiment results for the last benchmark run. What was the overall pass rate?
expected: Queries MLflow experiments for benchmark runs. Shows pass/fail counts, average scores, and overall pass rate.
criteria: Uses experiments endpoint. Reports aggregate metrics. Shows pass rate as percentage. Includes run details (model, timestamp).

input: Update the 'assistant' prompt to add a system instruction: 'Always respond in 3 sentences or fewer.' Show me the diff before applying.
expected: Gets current prompt, shows proposed change as a diff, asks for confirmation before updating.
criteria: Retrieves current version first. Shows clear diff. Does not auto-apply without showing changes. Mentions version increment.

input: What models are currently available through the inference proxy? Which one is the default?
expected: Lists models from configuration or LiteLLM. Identifies the default model. Shows model names and types (chat vs embedding).
criteria: Lists available models. Identifies default. Distinguishes chat from embedding models. Accurate to platform config.
